title: Melbourne Coffee Culture
extract: Melbourne's coffee culture is something you have to experience to believe. Every laneway hides a cafe where the barista takes their craft dead seriously. I watched a bloke spend four minutes adjusting his grinder between shots, muttering about humidity changes. The flat white was invented here—or in New Zealand, depending on who you ask, and I wouldn't bring that up at a Melbourne cafe unless you want a lecture. What makes it special isn't the coffee itself, it's the ritual. The morning queue at Market Lane, the afternoon espresso at Patricia, the way complete strangers bond over discussions about extraction time and milk texture.
